content.problemStatement:
Design a stack that supports the following operations in constant time: push, pop, top, and retrieving the minimum element.



Implement the MinStack class:



MinStack(): Initializes the stack object.

void push(int val): Pushes the element val onto the stack.


void pop(): removes the element on the top of the stack.


int top(): gets the top element of the stack.


int getMin(): retrieves the minimum element in the stack.
constraints: At most 10^4 calls will be made to push, pop, top and getMin
All the calls made to pop, top, and getMin are valid

hidden.label: tricky case / empty-like / negative if allowed

solution.approach: Use two stacks to keep track of the minimum element
solution.spaceComplexity: O(n) for storing the elements in the stack
